githubToolingMutationPolicy.purpose: Prevent agents from using full-file GitHub contents API replacement as the default for small repository edits.

coreRules.rule: When changing an existing file through GitHub tooling, prefer the smallest safe commit mechanism that preserves surrounding content.
Complete and auditable means specific, verified and traceable. It does not mean rewriting the whole file.
A user preference for full rewritten files applies to code shown in chat. It does not require full-file repository replacement for a small direct GitHub edit.

surgicalEdits.rule: For small changes inside an existing file, prefer a patch-capable or Git object workflow over contents-API full-file replacement.
Fetch the current file, blob or relevant line range before changing it.
Identify the exact changed region before selecting a write tool.

If using Git object primitives, create a corrected blob, create a tree based on the current branch head tree, create a commit with the current branch head as parent and update the branch ref with force=false.
Verify the resulting PR diff before reporting readiness.

contentsApiReplacement.rule: Do not use contents-API full-file replacement for large or complex source files unless the user explicitly requested a full file rewrite or the file is intentionally regenerated as a whole.

Only use contents-API full-file replacement when the file is short enough to review completely, the complete replacement has been generated and checked without placeholders and no patch-capable or Git object workflow is available.
If a full-file replacement is used, verify the changed-file list and file diff before saying the work is ready.

antiPatterns.rule: Do not repeatedly retry a blocked or failed full-file update_file operation.
After one blocked or failed full-file write, switch to a smaller Git object workflow, a patch-capable path or a clean branch strategy.
Do not create a tree from scratch for a normal repository edit unless intentionally creating a repository snapshot.
Do not use placeholders, omissions or summary tokens inside committed code, configuration or documentation.
Do not leave a known-bad PR open as ready for testing.

readinessGate.rule: Before reporting a PR as ready for review, testing or merge, verify the PR is open.
Verify the changed-file count is plausible for the task.
Verify the changed-file list contains only expected paths, or explain any extra paths.
Verify required trace files are linked when trace is required.
Close, supersede or clearly mark any known-bad predecessor PR as invalid.
